name: Fried Spring Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Green Onions, cut very fine
recipeInstructions:
1. In a wok or pan, heat 2 cups Oil over medium heat.
2. Add garlic and stir until fragrant, about 5 seconds.
3. Add the pork and shrimp and stir-fry for 1 minute.
4. Add the water chestnuts, carrots, celery, and green onions. Stir-fry for approximately 2 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender but still crunchy.
5. Add the soy sauce, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
6. Remove from heat. Drain excess moisture from the pan.
7. To assemble the rolls:
a. Place about 1/3 cup of the filling on the lower third of the wrapper.
b. Fold the portion of the wrapper nearest you over the filling until just covered.
c. Turn the wrapper again to enclose the filling securely.
d. Moisten the edges of the wrapper with water or a mixture of cornstarch and water.
e. Fold over the corners and press down firmly to seal, making an envelope.
f. Moisten the flap of the envelope with water and turn, rolling firmly into a 1-inch cylinder.
g. Seal firmly. Set aside, seam side down.
8. Repeat steps 7a-g until all the filling has been used.
9. Heat oil in a wok to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
10. Place 4 or 5 rolls at a time in the hot oil. Deep-fry until crisp and golden.
11. Remove with a skimmer and drain on paper towels.
12. Serve hot with Sweet and Sour Sauce.
Rolls may be prepared ahead of time and frozen, then fried when needed.
